data.title: ZAWYA: SlashData announces strategic partnership with Al Wathba Insurance
data.description:
• Supports Al Wathba Insurance’s modernization of motor insurance operations through the adoption of the Mulem platform
  • Partnership focuses on improving the insurer’s data access, processing efficiency, and underwriting accuracy

Abu Dhabi, UAE, SlashData, a UAE-based technology company specializing building national-scale digital platforms that connect the public and private sectors, has announced a strategic partnership with Al Wathba Insurance to support its modernization efforts through the adoption of Mulem, a platform designed to enable more efficient, intelligent, and data-driven motor insurance operations.

SlashData’s growing role in enabling the modernization of the insurance sector is reflected in this partnership with Al Wathba Insurance, which underscores the insurer’s focus on innovation, operational excellence, and digital leadership. Through the adoption of Mulem, Al Wathba Insurance is taking practical steps to modernize operations, strengthen risk management, and enhance efficiency across the motor insurance value chain.

Thamer Alfallaj, CEO of SlashData, said: “We are proud to partner with Al Wathba Insurance in advancing a more connected, secure, and intelligent insurance operating environment. As the insurance sector evolves, insurers that move toward trusted, integrated, and data‑driven models are better positioned to strengthen underwriting quality, accelerate operations, and reduce inefficiencies and risk exposure. Through Mulem, SlashData is enabling a more modern insurance ecosystem built on trusted data and stronger integration, and we believe this collaboration reflects the direction in which the sector is heading.”

Shukri Almheiri, Chief Executive Officer of Al Wathba Insurance, said: “At Al Wathba Insurance, our focus is not just on adopting technology, but on building a smarter, more resilient operating model for the future. Our partnership with SlashData and the adoption of Mulem is a strategic step towards enhancing data integrity, improving underwriting precision, and delivering a more efficient and seamless experience across our motor insurance operations.”

“We are committed to ensuring that every operational improvement translates into a better customer experience. With Mulem, we are simplifying processes, improving decision-making, and reducing friction across the motor insurance journey. This allows us to deliver on our promise of ‘Care Beyond Coverage’ in a more consistent and measurable way,” he added.

Developed and operated by SlashData, Mulem is a national digital integration platform designed to help insurers modernize motor insurance processes through secure, direct, and intelligent access to essential vehicle, driver, and accident-related data. Through direct system-to-system integration, the platform helps reduce reliance on manual processing, improves data accuracy, strengthens privacy protection, enhances controls against manipulation and fraud, supports fairer pricing, and reduces operational burden. The platform’s value proposition also includes faster transaction handling, stronger data governance, and more streamlined insurance operations overall.

Through this partnership, SlashData and Al Wathba Insurance aim to contribute to a stronger and more digitally enabled insurance ecosystem, one that supports operational resilience, improves service quality, and advances the sector’s overall maturity.

SlashData is a First.tech company and forms part of Judan Financial Holding, IHC’s dedicated financial services platform.

About SlashData:

SlashData is a leading UAE-based GovTech company specialized in building national-scale digital platforms that connect the public and private sectors. Through its advanced integration capabilities and AI-enabled infrastructure, SlashData supports government and regulated industries in improving interoperability, strengthening governance, and accelerating digital transformation.

SlashData has delivered digital solutions across mobility, insurance, and financial services, supporting key processes such as vehicle ownership transfer, financing, and insurance operations.

First.tech company forms part of Judan Financial Holding, IHC’s dedicated financial services platform.

data.title: Canada's Bombardier Q1 revenue misses estimates, EPS beats; backs FY rev outlook
data.description:
Overview

  • Canada business jet maker's Q1 revenue rose 5% yr/yr but missed analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 rose sharply and beat analyst expectations

  • Company raised 2026 free cash flow guidance to over $1.0 bln

Outlook

  • Bombardier raises 2026 free cash flow guidance to greater than $1.0 bln

  • Company reaffirms 2026 revenue guidance at $10.0 bln and adjusted EBITDA at $1.625 bln

  • Bombardier expects continued strong demand, supported by a $20.3 bln backlog

Result Drivers

  • SERVICES GROWTH - 25% yr/yr increase in Services revenue to $617 mln drove overall revenue growth

  • ORDER MOMENTUM - Strong demand from fleet operators and for Global 8000 boosted book-to-bill to 3.6x and backlog to $20.3 bln

  • FREE CASH FLOW SWING - Free cash flow rose by $664 mln yr/yr, driven by positive cash flows from operating activities

data.title: Trane Technologies Plc Profit Declines In Q1

The company's bottom line came in at $594.4 million, or $2.66 per share. This compares with $613.8 million, or $2.71 per share, last year.

The company's revenue for the period rose 6.0% to $4.96 billion from $4.68 billion last year.

data.title: Kone Deal Structure for TK Elevator Buy Is Better Than Expected — Market Talk
data.description: Kone's deal structure for the acquisition of TK Elevator seems better than expected, Citi analyst Vivek Midha writes. The price is within the range Citi had expected that still enables EPS accretion, while no rights issue removes some risk for public investors. Antitrust approval remains a key item going forward though, the analyst writes. Kone announced an agreement to acquire TK Elevator in a deal valued at 29.4 billion euros, including debt. The cash and stock deal is guided to be accretive to EPS in year one, with an accelerating trajectory. This is driven by 700 million euros in pretax synergies by year three. The pro forma net debt-to-Ebitda ratio of 4 is above Citi's assumption, but not that concerning given the cash-generative profile of the group. Kone shares fall 0.4%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Unilever food troubles vindicate beauty makeover
data.description:
By Aimee Donnellan

The $125 bln group's mayonnaise-to-stock-cubes unit was the weak spot in its results. It comes alongside problems at private equity-owned spreads and tea brands, which the company sold. It all strengthens the case for a planned food exit and greater focus on health and beauty.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Aimee Donnellan on LinkedIn.

CONTEXT NEWS

Unilever's first-quarter underlying sales growth beat analysts' forecasts on April 30, driven by increased volumes in emerging markets and demand for its "power brands", including Dove soap and Vaseline. The British company posted underlying sales growth of 3.8% in the three months to March, ahead of the 3.6% growth expected by analysts in a company-compiled consensus.

The volume rise was led by so-called power brands, which include Dove, Axe deodorant and Dermalogica skincare. They grew underlying sales by 5%, with 4% volume growth.

Magnum Ice Cream, the business spun out of Unilever in 2025, also reported results on April 30. It said that organic sales growth was 4.5% in the first quarter. The company said it was on track to deliver 3% to 5% sales growth in 2026.

Shares in Magnum were up 11.3% by 0810 GMT on April 30.

KKR is exploring a sale of a spreads business, which includes Flora, that it bought from Unilever, the Financial Times reported on April 30.

CVC is putting 210 million euros into the tea business it bought from Unilever, according to a Financial Times report on April 29.

data.title: No more 'good place'
data.description:
Key points:
  • STOXX 600 up 0.4%
  • Busy earnings day, French banks struggle, Rolls Royce gains
  • ECB and BoE meetings to come
  • U.S. futures steady

NO MORE 'GOOD PLACE'

European Central Bank policymakers might once again soon be performing a balancing act as the economic picture grows increasingly tricky.

Euro zone inflation jumped to 3% in April from 2.6% in March, flash data showed, with energy prices largely driving the increase. On the brighter side, any potential second-round effects from higher energy prices don’t seem to have kicked in yet.

But of course they can’t be excluded. For example food prices may rise due to fertiliser shortages, and the impact of energy prices on headline inflation will likely increase even more, Peter Vanden Houte, chief economist for Belgium, Luxembourg and the Eurozone at ING, noted.

“Inflation is likely to creep higher towards the 4% level,” he said.

Meanwhile, euro zone economic growth was faint in the first quarter, data also showed on Thursday.

That leaves the ECB in a difficult situation.

In fact, the data points “suggest that the ECB has shifted from being 'in a good place' in early 2026 to facing a brutal stagflation shock,” said Claus Vistesen, chief eurozone economist at Pantheon Macroeconomics.

data.title: Valero Energy beats estimates for first-quarter profit
data.description:
U.S. refiner Valero Energy surpassed Wall Street expectations for first-quarter profit on Thursday, helped by higher margins.

The San Antonio, Texas-based company posted an adjusted profit of $4.22 per share for the three months ended March 31, compared with analysts' expectations of $3.16 per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

data.title: Twenty One Outlines Plan to Acquire Strike and Elektron, Targeting Mining, Finance & Securitization
data.description:
Twenty One outlined an operating strategy centered on potential acquisitions of Strike and Elektron to build integrated Bitcoin financial services and mining infrastructure.

Key Highlights:

  • Announced potential acquisitions of Strike (Bitcoin financial services) and Elektron (large-scale Bitcoin mining) to consolidate capabilities.
  • Strategy focuses on four pillars: financial services & distribution, Bitcoin infrastructure, capital markets, and mergers & acquisitions.
  • Capital markets plan includes future securitization of loan books and mining revenue and using treasury-backed, non-dilutive leverage.
  • Objective is to move beyond treasury holdings to an integrated platform anchored by recurring revenue and long-term Bitcoin accumulation.
  • Presentation materials, press release, and transcript from the CEO's Bitcoin 2026 Conference talk are furnished as exhibits.

Original SEC Filing: Twenty One Capital, Inc. [ XXI ] - 8-K - Apr. 30, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Royal Caribbean Group posts Q1 Adjusted EPS $3.60, Revenues $4.45B and raises full-year Adjusted EPS view
data.description:
Royal Caribbean Group reported first-quarter 2026 results with total revenue of $4.45 billion and Adjusted diluted EPS of $3.60, beating prior guidance. Net income attributable to Royal Caribbean Group was $941 million (diluted EPS $3.48) and Adjusted EBITDA was $1.7 billion. The company updated full-year 2026 Adjusted EPS guidance to $17.10–$17.50 and noted stronger-than-expected demand alongside higher fuel cost assumptions.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $4,452 million for the quarter (up 11% year over year).
  • Gross Margin (as reported): Gross Margin $1,744 million; Gross Margin Yields $127.29 per APCD.
  • Operating income: $1,162 million for the quarter.
  • Net income attributable to Royal Caribbean Group: $941 million; Diluted EPS $3.48.
  • Adjusted measures: Adjusted Net Income attributable $975 million; Adjusted diluted EPS $3.60; Adjusted EBITDA $1,702 million.

Business Highlights

  • Strong demand environment: Record WAVE season and continued strength in close-in bookings drove higher pricing and onboard revenue growth.
  • Capacity and guest volumes: First-quarter capacity up 8% year over year, serving 2.5 million guests (12% increase) with APCD of 13.7 million and load factor ~109%.
  • Bookings dynamic: Mediterranean and West Coast of Mexico itineraries moderated in late Q1 due to geopolitical and air-service disruptions but have largely rebounded and are running ahead of last year for remaining inventory.
  • Portfolio expansion and destinations: Launched Royal Beach Club Santorini, expects delivery of Legend of the Seas in Q2, announced orders for Icon VI and Icon VII, and continues expansion of private destinations and the Royal ONE credit card to deepen guest engagement.
  • Liquidity and capital actions: Liquidity of $6.9 billion as of March 31, 2026; issued $2.5 billion of senior notes and repurchased 2.9 million shares for $836 million with $1.0 billion remaining repurchase authorization.

Original SEC Filing: ROYAL CARIBBEAN CRUISES LTD [ RCL ] - 8-K - Apr. 30, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Donegal Group reports Q1 2026: Net income $11.5M, revenue $236.0M, combined ratio 99.8%
data.description:
Donegal Group reported first-quarter 2026 results with total revenues of $236.0 million and net income of $11.5 million, or $0.31 per diluted Class A share. The company reported a GAAP combined ratio of 99.8% (vs. 91.6% year-ago) and non-GAAP operating income of $11.9 million. Book value per share rose to $17.54 at March 31, 2026.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $235.996 million for the three months ended March 31, 2026.
  • Net income: $11.511 million; diluted net income per Class A share: $0.31.
  • Non-GAAP operating income: $11.889 million (after-tax adjustment for investment losses).
  • GAAP combined ratio: 99.8% for Q1 2026, compared with 91.6% for Q1 2025.
  • Book value per share: $17.54 at March 31, 2026 (up from $16.24 at March 31, 2025).

Business Highlights

  • Net premiums earned decreased 4.9% to $221.357 million year-over-year for Q1 2026.
  • Net premiums written declined 3.2% to $239.290 million, driven by a 13.1% decrease in personal lines offset by a 2.2% increase in commercial lines.
  • Commercial lines showed growth in net premiums written (up 2.2%), led by automobile and other commercial categories and solid retention.
  • Personal lines continued to contract (personal net premiums written down 13.1%), with automobile and homeowners declines cited as drivers.
  • Underwriting performance note: elevated weather-related losses ($17.2 million) and higher large fire losses ($12.2 million) increased the loss ratio; core loss ratios for both commercial and personal segments improved modestly versus prior year.

Original SEC Filing: DONEGAL GROUP INC [ DGICA ] - 8-K - Apr. 30, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.
